record.title: Henry Benson, 1974-05-15, Interview - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.keywords: African Americans holidays homesteads Native Americans religion railroads IWW unions world wars mills illness illness schools churches

point.title: Joe Wells influence; Halloween pranks
point.synopsis: Joe Wells and family. The kids put cow manure over the schoolhouse door as a Halloween prank. Lou's snoose and tobacco. The Wells schoolhouse was also the church.

point.title: Getting by in the early days
point.synopsis: Getting by in the early days: making lunches for the railroad crews, and growing food. Norwegians around Deary. Caskets. Ice for Magnuson's meat market.

point.title: Clearing the homestead
point.synopsis: Clearing the homestead. Girdling trees. Using scythes and cradles.

point.title: Relationship with Bjerke family
point.synopsis: Old man Bjerke had a homemade lock on his granary. He worked with a grubhoe when he was old. Catching trout with Arthur Bjerke on the little Potlatch.

point.title: Pneumonia almost kills Henry as a child
point.synopsis: Henry almost dies from pneumonia as a boy.

point.title: Trading with Native Americans; camas picking; mineral springs
point.synopsis: Giving hides to the Indian bands that passed through. They picked camas between Helmer and Bovill on the meadows. Mineral springs.

point.title: Railroad engine
point.synopsis: Firing the little engine on the railroad.

point.title: Railroad stations
point.synopsis: The stations on the railroad run.

point.title: Relationship with bums and the train
point.synopsis: Henry's orders were to kick "bums" off the train, but then he was yelled at for running behind schedule. The bums had spots in Palouse, Potlatch and Bovill to "mulligan up", and slept on the cars; Henry liked them.

point.title: Building the railroad with horses
point.synopsis: Pile driving with a horse team to build the railroad.

point.title: Engine conversion from slobber stacks to superheaters
point.synopsis: Converting the engines from slobber stacks to superheaters- this meant the engines needed much less water. Running checks on the engines.

point.title: Train derailment reviewed by Potlatch
point.synopsis: An 18 car derailment caused by a log rolling of at a switch at Vassar. Management from Potlatch views the damage.

point.title: Problems with bridges; refusal to cross them
point.synopsis: He refuses to go over Bridge 10 after the sway braces crack; or Bridge 18 after a mud slide, which took six days to fix. Bridge 13 was bad.

point.title: IWW and labor unions
point.synopsis: Engineer and section crews both paid 27 cents an hour around 1920. Unions forced the pay up. The IWWs straightened out the camps.

point.title: Drinking apple cider after enlisting for World War I
point.synopsis: After he enlists during World War I, the Moscow sheriff (Jab Campbell) takes the Deary enlistees to Troy to get drunk on apple cider.

point.title: Freighting with Joe Wells
point.synopsis: Freighting with his father and Joe Wells for Camp 6 with sleighs.

record.description: With Nina Seybold (sister) Unsafe railroad bridges; a major derailment. Support of IWWs and hoboes. Family homesteading. Joe Wells family. 5-15-74 1 hr
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
